original_abstract: Characterizing the biochemical composition of *Rosa canina* L. fruits remains a subject of significant academic and practical interest, as evidenced by a burgeoning body of research. Renowned for their high concentrations of ascorbic acid, phenolics, and flavonoids, these fruits are increasingly recognized for their potent antioxidant, antimutagenic, and anticarcinogenic properties. This study was conducted to quantify the primary phytochemical constituents—specifically vitamin C, total polyphenols, and total flavonoids—and to evaluate their associated antioxidant potential.
humanized_abstract: The biochemical characterization of Rsa canina L. fruits is an increasingly expanding subject in the scientific literature. The fruits of Rsa canina L. are well-known sources of ascorbic acid, phenolics and flavonoids, and are rapidly becoming increasingly well characterised for their antioxidant, antimutagenic and anticarcinogenic activities. The aim of the present study was to evaluate and quantify the vitamin C, total polyphenols, total flavonoids of Rsa canina L. fruits and their antioxidant activity.

document.output: The biochemical characterization of Rsa canina L. fruits is an increasingly expanding subject in the scientific literature. The fruits of Rsa canina L. are well-known sources of ascorbic acid, phenolics and flavonoids, and are rapidly becoming increasingly well characterised for their antioxidant, antimutagenic and anticarcinogenic activities. The aim of the present study was to evaluate and quantify the vitamin C, total polyphenols, total flavonoids of Rsa canina L. fruits and their antioxidant activity.
